studyName: Resource for Genetic Epidemiology Research on Adult Health and Aging (GERA)

tables.description: This GERA subject phenotype table includes chip IDs and principal components scores determined for the following self-reported race/ethnicity/nationality from the RPGEH survey: African Americans (AFR), East Asians (EAS), non-Hispanic White Europeans (EUR), Latinos (LAT), and South Asians. Principal components analysis was used to characterize genetic structure in this multi-ethnic sample.

This GERA subject phenotype table includes disease and conditions variables based on ICD-9-CM. Diagnoses were obtained from patient encounters at Kaiser Permanente Northern California facilities from January 1, 1995 to March 15, 2013. The participant was coded with disease if the participant had at least two diagnoses in a disease category recorded.

variables.name: BMICAT
variables.description: Body Mass Index of participant, based on clinical data, at time point closest to date of specimen sample submission.

variables.name: EDUCAT
variables.description: Self-reported education level at date of survey completion.

variables.name: GENERALHEALTHCAT
variables.description: Self-reported health status at date of survey completion.

variables.name: INCOMECAT
variables.description: Self-reported income level at date of survey completion.

variables.name: KPMEMBERSHIPYEAR
variables.description: First year of membership in Kaiser Permanente Northern California Health Plan, with dates beginning in 1980 and ending in 2005.  All members with enrollment post-2005 were hard-coded to 2005 (N=1).

variables.name: MARITALCAT
variables.description: Self-reported marital status at date of survey completion.

variables.name: PHYSICALACTIVITYCAT
variables.description: Physical activity Total Metabolic Equivalency of Task (MET) quartile categories, 4 categories per gender.  Total reflects the summation of questions on walking, moderate and vigorous activity levels.  Quartiles based on distribution of complete RPGEH survey cohort, not dbGaP subset.

variables.name: SMOKINGPACKYEARSCAT
variables.description: Calculation of packyears for all survey respondents who answered "former" (2) or "current" (3) to SmokingStatusCat.

variables.name: SMOKINGSTATUSCAT
variables.description: Self-reported smoking status at date of survey completion.

tables.description: This GERA subject phenotype table includes survey variables collected from 3 sources: 1) Kaiser Permanente Northern California's Research Program on Genes, Environment and Health Survey: conducted 2007 - present; 2) Kaiser Permanente's California Men's Health Study (Northern California subset): conducted 2001 - 2004; 3) Kaiser Pernamente Northern California Patient Electronic Medical Record (EMR): current. Variables includes Kaiser Permanente Enrollment year, sociodemography (n=6 variables; birth year, education, income, marital status, race, and gender), physical observations (n=3 variables; BMI, general health, and physical activity), lifestyle and environment (n=3 variables; alcohol use, smoking status and use).

Total reflects the summation of questions on walking, moderate and vigorous activity levels.  Quartiles based on distribution of complete RPGEH survey cohort, not dbGaP subset.

variables.name: TWIN_ID
variables.description: Unique ID per set of siblings.

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

tables.description: This GERA sample attributes table includes body site where sample was extracted, analyte type, package number, Affymetrix kit type, and second pass call rate.

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected
